title: Rhaphiolepis laoshanica
intro: Rhaphiolepis laoshanica, also known as the Chinese hawthorn, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to China, belonging to the family Rosaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and cardiovascular-protective properties, which may help to reduce cholesterol levels, improve digestion, and lower blood pressure. Rhaphiolepis laoshanica contains a range of bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, triterpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Rhaphiolepis laoshanica is often used to make herbal preparations, such as decoctions and teas, as well as being used in combination with other herbs to treat various health conditions.
